meaning: adj.向着海岸的,陆上的adv.向着海岸,在陆上
examples: Food onshore is very limited and there is simply nothing comparable to a seal that provides a polar bear with fat and energy.
They missed the ferry and remained onshore.
In Cayman, this firm would not be taxed, but onshore it might have been.
Note that companies specializing in the Gulf of Mexico tend to trade at discounts to onshore firms.
Without it, the onshore economy would not even have had a double dip recession last year.
